generation_steps: 1. Resolve role from npc_roles by role_id.
2. Validate settlement_type is allowed for role, otherwise pick nearest compatible role fallback.
3. Resolve anchor template from npc_anchor_templates using role.default_anchor_template.
4. Build resolved stats from anchor base_stats.
5. Apply modifier stat adjustments in order.
6. Clamp final abilities to sane range (minimum 3, maximum 20 for MVP).
7. Recompute derived values if needed (AC, HP, passive perception if your system supports it).
8. Merge default actions, equipment, and spellcasting from anchor.
10. Add role-specific possible skills or trait tags if not already present.
11. Roll or select motivation, secret, and rumor from role pools.
12. Generate name from naming library using species/culture tables.
13. Generate short description and portrait prompt using species + role + modifiers + biome.
npc_generation_schema.portrait_prompt_template: {name}, a {modifier_adjectives} {species_origin} {role_name} from the {biome_name}. Fantasy portrait, readable silhouette, clear face, detailed clothing and gear, dungeon-born aesthetic, centered composition, no text. Visual details: {visual_keywords}. Mood: {mood_keywords}.
npc_generation_schema.short_description_template: {name} is a {role_name} based in a {settlement_type} near the {biome_name}. They come across as {social_keywords} and are driven to {motivation}.

fallback_rules.invalid_role_for_settlement: fallback to nearest role in same category allowed by settlement
